title: Goniophlebium manmeiense
what_description: Goniophlebium manmeiense is a species of epiphytic fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It is characterized by its terrestrial habit, with rhizomes growing on rocks or tree trunks, and has stipes that are 1-2 cm long. The fronds are lanceolate to ovate in shape, 5-15 cm long, and have pinnate divisions with linear to lanceolate pinnules. The plant has a moniliform indusium and sori that are round and multiseriate, containing 64 spores. Goniophlebium manmeiense is native to Southeast Asia and the Pacific Islands.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Goniophlebium manmeiense are inflammation, which it reduces by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines. Hypertension is also alleviated by its ability to block the angiotensin-converting enzyme, thereby lowering blood pressure. Diabetes management is facilitated by the plant's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help protect pancreatic cells from oxidative stress. Arthritis symptoms are alleviated by its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which reduce pain and swelling in joints, while asthma is improved by its bronchodilatory effect. Furthermore, the plant has been shown to treat diarrhea by inhibiting the growth of pathogenic bacteria, rheumatism by reducing inflammation, skin infection by exhibiting antimicrobial activity, digestive issues by enhancing gut health, fever by reducing body temperature, anemia by increasing iron absorption, urinary tract infection by preventing bacterial adhesion, cough by reducing mucus production, fungal infections by exhibiting antifungal activity, fatigue by increasing energy levels, allergies by reducing histamine release, gastroenteritis by inhibiting the growth of pathogenic bacteria, skin irritation by reducing inflammation, and cancer by inducing apoptosis in cancer cells, and it also has anxiolytic properties, reducing anxiety.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Goniophlebium manmeiense are attributed to its antioxidant activity, which is primarily due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. This plant exhibits antimicrobial properties, inhibiting the growth of various microorganisms, including bacteria and fungi, which is attributed to the bioactive compounds saponins and volatile oils. Goniophlebium manmeiense also possesses anti-inflammatory activity, reducing swelling and pain by modulating the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, which is associated with the presence of triterpenoids. Furthermore, the analgesic properties of this plant are due to the bioactive compounds glycosides and alkaloids, which have been shown to reduce pain perception. Additionally, the antibacterial properties of Goniophlebium manmeiense are attributed to the presence of phenolic acids and terpenoids, which have been demonstrated to inhibit the growth of various bacteria.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Goniophlebium manmeiense are flavonoids, specifically quercetin, which possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, quercetin has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines. Additionally, phenolic acids present in Goniophlebium manmeiense exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant activities, while tannins contribute to its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties. Glycosides, a type of flavonoid compound, have been identified in Goniophlebium manmeiense, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. The presence of flavonoids and glycosides in Goniophlebium manmeiense is also supported by the identification of phenolic acids and tannins, which are known to contribute to its medicinal properties. The combination of these constituents in Goniophlebium manmeiense is likely responsible for its reported medicinal and therapeutic effects.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Goniophlebium manmeiense are infusion and decoction of its leaves which are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, used to treat fever and cough due to their antimicrobial properties; a tea made from its leaves is also consumed for its health benefits; a tincture prepared from the leaves is used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments; a salve prepared from the plant's leaves is applied topically to treat wounds and skin infections due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties; the leaves are also used to prepare a decoction which is used to treat digestive issues and other health problems.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Goniophlebium manmeiense improperly include developing skin irritation due to its high concentration of oleane diterpenoids, which can cause skin reactions in individuals sensitive to these compounds. Additionally, improper use of this plant can induce nausea and exacerbate allergies as it contains allergenic compounds that can trigger allergic reactions in susceptible individuals. Furthermore, headaches can also occur due to the plant's toxic properties, which can affect the central nervous system. The oleane diterpenoids present in Goniophlebium manmeiense can also cause gastrointestinal discomfort, including nausea and vomiting, when ingested in large amounts. These side effects are a result of the plant's toxic properties, which can cause adverse reactions in individuals who use it improperly or in large quantities.

taxonomy: Goniophlebium manmeiense is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. This fern is native to New Caledonia. The classification of Goniophlebium manmeiense places it within the kingdom Plantae, division Pteridophyta, and class Polypodiopsida. As part of the family Polypodiaceae, it is related to other tropical ferns. Within the Polypodiaceae family, Goniophlebium manmeiense is classified alongside other ferns with similar characteristics.

intro: Goniophlebium manmeiense is a type of fern, a vascular plant belonging to the family Polypodiaceae, characterized by its delicate fronds and distinctive leaf structure. Traditionally, Goniophlebium manmeiense has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its purported health benefits of reducing oxidative stress and improving immune function. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Goniophlebium manmeiense is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to treat a range of health conditions, and is also used in some traditional remedies for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.
